title: Fix return type of bytes.join()
body:
This was already tested in `tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx`, but there were apparently (non-trivial?) cases where the return type wasn't correctly inferred.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Ensure Cython always infers `bytes.join()` as returning a `bytes` object by fixing both builtin method typing (signature and inference helper) and any optimisation rewrites that currently lose the precise return type. Extend coverage as needed to catch previously-missed call shapes and confirm inference remains `bytes object`.

steps.operation: Adjust builtin method return-type inference so `bytes.join` is consistently inferred as returning `bytes` across call forms (including less-direct/special-cased paths)
steps.reason: Some compilation/optimisation paths may not rely on the builtin method’s C-signature return code and instead consult (or should consult) the centralized return-type inference helper. Ensuring `bytes.join` maps to `Builtin.bytes_type` (or receiver type `T` resolved to bytes) here provides a single authoritative source for inference when signature-based typing is bypassed.
steps.side_effects: May change inferred types for any call paths that consult this helper (potentially improving type precision). Could affect downstream optimisations/codegen decisions and might expose latent assumptions where code expected `object`.

steps.operation: Fix the builtin-method declaration for `bytes.join` so its `CFuncType.return_type` is `bytes` rather than generic `object` (align with `str.join`)
steps.reason: If `bytes.join` is declared with return code `"O"`, any typing that uses the method signature directly will infer a generic Python object. Updating the declaration logic/table usage so `bytes.join` returns `"T"` (receiver type) or explicitly `Builtin.bytes_type` ensures all signature-driven inference yields `bytes` and matches runtime semantics.
steps.side_effects: Can affect specialised-call generation and C-API wrapper selection when return types drive coercions or temp management. Requires checking that other bytes methods are unaffected and that `str.join` remains unchanged.

steps.operation: Audit and update optimisation rewrites for `.join()` (and related call-pattern rewrites encountered during loop/iteration optimisation) to preserve/set the resulting expression node type to `bytes` when optimising `bytes.join`
steps.reason: Optimiser rewrites can replace a Python-level method call with a specialised node/C-API call and accidentally drop precise typing (defaulting to `py_object_type`). Ensuring that join-related rewritten nodes explicitly carry `Builtin.bytes_type` (or propagate the inferred return type from the original call) prevents regressions in “non-trivial” call shapes that take optimised paths.
steps.side_effects: May alter which optimised code paths are selected and the generated C code’s coercion/temporary handling. Needs regression testing to ensure no semantic changes for error paths (TypeError, non-bytes inputs) and no impact on `str.join` optimisation.
